question_text: A microwave uses 2000 J of electrical energy to heat some food. If 1500 J of energy is transferred to the food as heat, what is the efficiency of the microwave?
explanation: Efficiency = useful energy output ÷ total energy input × 100%. The useful output is 1500 J (heat to food) and total input is 2000 J, so 1500 ÷ 2000 × 100 = 75%. This is a relatively high efficiency because most of the electrical energy reaches the food.
